assertions.title: Drag-and-drop a valid PDF triggers processing

steps.action: Open app on drop screen and capture baseline
steps.observed: Drop zone rendered with browse link and mode selector

steps.action: Drag-drop a valid application/pdf file onto .drop zone
steps.expected: File accepted and app leaves drop screen for processing flow
steps.observed: File accepted and app transitioned into review flow (processing state was transient)

assertions.title: Click anywhere on drop zone opens file picker

steps.action: Instrument input[type=file] click counter, then click icon, label, spec text, and browse link
steps.expected: Each click opens file picker trigger
steps.observed: input click counter incremented to 4 (one for each region clicked)

steps.action: Drop a .txt file onto the drop zone
steps.expected: Error shown and app stays on drop screen
steps.observed: Message shown: 'Only PDF files are supported. Please select a valid PDF.' and drop screen remained

assertions.title: Reject file exceeding 50 MB size limit

steps.action: Drop a 51MB application/pdf file onto the drop zone
steps.observed: Message shown: 'File exceeds the 50 MB size limit. Please select a smaller PDF.' and app remained on drop screen

assertions.title: Error state clears on subsequent valid drop

steps.action: Drop non-PDF to trigger error, then drop valid PDF
steps.expected: Previous error clears and valid file processes
steps.observed: UI transitioned to preview/review after valid drop and prior drop-screen error text no longer present

steps.action: Drop two PDF files in a single drop event
steps.expected: Graceful handling (one-file-only behavior/message) and no crash
steps.observed: Message shown: 'Please drop one file at a time.' and app stayed functional on drop screen

frictions.description: For tiny valid PDFs, the processing screen was too brief to reliably capture before review rendered.
frictions.resolution: Confirmed acceptance by verifying immediate transition out of drop screen into review flow and captured after-drop screenshot there.

summary: Tested 6 assertions: 6 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Drop/upload validation behavior matched contract for valid files, invalid type, oversize, recovery, and multi-file handling.
